lines: Ellis leads the pack on 13pts but her New Zealand side just got absolutely mullered 3-1 by Egypt — love that for her, truly poetic stuff from a team that were basically there to make up the numbers.
Jim's Spain put FOUR past Wilmot's Saudi Arabia yesterday, which means Jim is cackling at 12pts while Wilmot watches his tournament slowly dissolve into a fine mist of broken dreams and false hope.

Today's Group I double-header is basically a sweepstake orgy — France vs Iraq involves Callum, Croft, Dene AND The Foreman, while Norway vs Senegal has five cunts with a stake in it, so someone's getting a result and someone's getting absolutely nothing.
Dene is sitting on 4pts at the bottom of the table with France, Senegal, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Tunisia — mate, that's not a sweepstake draw, that's a hostage situation.

Keep your eyes on Carter today — Algeria vs Jordan in Group J, both teams on zero points and one game played, meaning his Algeria are already in must-win territory while Ellis's Argentina are strutting around the top of the group like they own the fucking place.
